problem_statement: Fully automating code review to save developer time risks losing the interpersonal and team benefits of the practice; the paper argues AI should support (keep humans in the loop on) code review rather than replace it.
magnitude_evidence: developers spending 10–20% of their working time doing code reviews; with ~28 million developers in 2024 this corresponds to 22-44 million hours every work day
insufficiency_evidence: while automated tools might succeed in maintaining the code quality, we risk losing interpersonal and team benefits such as knowledge transfer, shared code ownership, and team awareness
solution_mechanisms: AI-OS architecture with central LLM kernel
specialized small agents for tool/data integration
adaptive pipeline customized to user needs
